title: TypeScript Reference
description: Complete TypeScript types, interfaces, and generics reference.

items.name: any
items.description: Disables type checking for a variable.

items.name: void
items.description: Indicates a function does not return a value.

items.name: null / undefined
items.code:
let u: undefined = undefined;
let n: null = null;

items.name: Array
items.description: Typed arrays using generics or bracket syntax.
items.code:
let nums: number[] = [1, 2, 3];
let names: Array<string> = ['a', 'b'];

items.name: Tuple
items.description: Fixed-length array with specific types per index.

items.name: interface
items.description: Defines the shape of an object.

items.name: readonly
extends
Generic Function
items.description: Create reusable functions with type parameters.

items.name: Constraints
Required<T>
items.code: const full: Required<User> = { name: 'A', age: 1, email: 'a@b.c' };

items.name: Pick<T, K>
items.description: Select specific properties from a type.

items.name: Omit<T, K>
items.description: Exclude specific properties from a type.

items.name: Record<K, V>
items.description: Create a type with specified keys and value type.
items.code: const scores: Record<string, number> = { math: 95, eng: 88 };
